question: Implement a `DynamicIntegerArray` class that manages a dynamically allocated array of integers. Its constructor should take an integer `size` and allocate memory for an array of that size. Implement a destructor that correctly deallocates the dynamically allocated memory to prevent memory leaks.

test_cases.input: DynamicIntegerArray originalArr(3); originalArr.set(0, 10); originalArr.set(1, 20); originalArr.set(2, 30); DynamicIntegerArray copiedArr = originalArr; copiedArr.set(0, 100); originalArr.print(); copiedArr.print();
test_cases.expected:
Original Array: [10, 20, 30]
Copied Array: [100, 20, 30]
(No crash upon destruction)

errors: The copy constructor performs a shallow copy of the `data` pointer instead of a deep copy. This results in two `DynamicIntegerArray` objects managing the same memory block. When both objects are destroyed, their destructors will attempt to `delete[]` the same memory twice, leading to a double-free, which is undefined behavior and typically causes a program crash.
